_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Ability to identify systemic security issues based on the analysis of vulnerability and configuration data.
reference_document_element_description: Ability to diagnose and resolve technical issues that span multiple system layers, tracing problems across application code, data pipelines, databases, networks, and infrastructure to identify and remediate root causes.
comments: DCWF ksa intersects with WIDAI TF domain. TF-A-002 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: TF-S-012 (TF, cosine: 0.465), DQ-A-002 (DQ, cosine: 0.465).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 5.9/10 (raw 0.587). Shared vocabulary: [ability, data, identify, issues]. DCWF-only terms [analysis, based, configuration, security, systemic, vulnerability] vs WIDAI-only terms [across, application, causes, code, databases, diagnose] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.884 (P=0.901, R=0.868). P exceeds R by 0.034 — the DCWF element's 15 words are well-captured by the WIDAI KSA's 29 words, but the KSA contains additional specificity not reflected in the DCWF element.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: mixed signal (E:0.5%, N:78.9%, C:20.5%). No dominant classification for "Ability to identify systemic security issues based on the analysis of vulnerabil" vs "Ability to diagnose and resolve technical issues that span multiple system layer" — the relationship is genuinely ambiguous from a logical inference perspective.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.526 < STS 0.587 (gap -0.061). Cross-attention finds deeper alignment than independent encoding. The relationship depends on cross-text reasoning — concept-level mapping between [analysis, based, configuration] and [across, application, causes] that bi-encoders structurally cannot capture.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 17.1%. Shared tokens: [ability, data, identify, issues]. DCWF-only: [analysis, based, configuration, security, systemic, vulnerability]. WIDAI-only: [across, application, causes, code, databases, diagnose].
